---
title: "STAT 2020 Discussion Slides"
subtitle: "Week 7: Poisson, Integration, and Continuous Distributions"
author: "TA"
institute: "University of Iowa"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
bibliography: refs.bib
output: 
  beamer_presentation:
    theme: "Warsaw"
    colortheme: "uiowa"
    keep-tex: true
    slide_level: 3 # can toggle this to 2 if subsections are not needed
    incremental: true 
    includes: 
      in_header: 
        - ./custom.tex
header-includes:
   - \AtBeginDocument{\title[STAT 2020 Week 7\hspace{18em} \insertframenumber\ of \inserttotalframenumber]{STAT 2020 Discussion Slides}}
   - \logo{\includegraphics[width=0.12\textwidth]{iowa-logo.png}}
---

# Week 7 Goals

## Agenda

### This Discussion Covers

  - Show how Poisson means change with interval/region size
  - Review core integration methods
  - Work one piecewise continuous example:
    - CDF from PDF
    - PDF from CDF
    - \(\Ex(X)\) and \(\Var(X)\)
  - Class policy reminder on exam-question discussions

# Poisson Rate and Window Size

## Time-Interval Example

### Call Center Arrivals

Assume arrivals follow \(N_t \sim \Pois(\lambda t)\).

If the average is 3 calls per 10 minutes, then:

\[
\lambda = 0.3 \text{ calls/minute}
\]

So expected counts scale with interval length:

  - 5 minutes: \(\Ex(N_5) = 0.3 \times 5 = 1.5\)
  - 20 minutes: \(\Ex(N_{20}) = 0.3 \times 20 = 6\)
  - 1 hour: \(\Ex(N_{60}) = 18\)

## Region-Size Example

### Spatial Poisson Process

Suppose emergency events occur at rate \(\rho = 2\) per square mile per day.

For area \(A\):
\[
N_A \sim \Pois(\rho A), \quad \Ex(N_A) = \rho A
\]

  - \(A=1\): \(\Ex(N_A)=2\)
  - \(A=3.5\): \(\Ex(N_A)=7\)
  - \(A=10\): \(\Ex(N_A)=20\)

# Integration Methods (Quick Review)

## Core Techniques

### Method Checklist

  - Substitution
  - Integration by parts
  - Partial fractions
  - Symmetry tricks
  - Piecewise integration
  - Optional advanced method (skip if time is tight)

## Key Identities

### Useful Formulas

\[
\int f(g(x))g'(x)\,dx = \int f(u)\,du
\]

\[
\int u\,dv = uv - \int v\,du
\]

# Continuous Piecewise Example

## Define a PDF

### Two Non-Zero Segments

\[
f_X(x)=
\begin{cases}
x, & 0<x\le 1,\\
2-x, & 1<x<2,\\
0, & \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
\]

This is a continuous-distribution example with \(f(X=x)=0\) for every single point \(x\).

## Derive CDF from PDF

### Piecewise Integration

\[
F_X(x)=
\begin{cases}
0, & x\le 0,\\
\frac{x^2}{2}, & 0<x\le 1,\\
1-\frac{(2-x)^2}{2}, & 1<x<2,\\
1, & x\ge 2.
\end{cases}
\]

## Recover PDF from CDF

### Differentiate by Interval

\[
f_X(x)=F'_X(x)=
\begin{cases}
x, & 0<x<1,\\
2-x, & 1<x<2,\\
0, & \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
\]

At isolated points (e.g., \(x=1\)), the exact point value does not change probabilities.

## Compute \(\Ex(X)\)

### Mean

\[
\Ex(X)=\int_0^1 x\cdot x\,dx + \int_1^2 x(2-x)\,dx = 1
\]

## Compute \(\Var(X)\)

### Variance

\[
\Ex(X^2)=\int_0^1 x^2\cdot x\,dx + \int_1^2 x^2(2-x)\,dx = \frac{7}{6}
\]

\[
\Var(X)=\Ex(X^2)-\Ex(X)^2=\frac{7}{6}-1=\frac{1}{6}
\]

# Class Reminder

## Exam Questions Policy

### What to Say in Discussion

  - Do not discuss specific exam questions in class.
  - If students ask, invite them to office hours.
  - Suggested response: "I cannot discuss exam details in class, but I can help during office hours."

## Take home messages 

  - In Poisson models, means scale linearly with time/region size.
  - Use systematic piecewise integration for CDF/PDF/\(\Ex\)/\(\Var\) tasks.
  - Keep exam-specific conversations in office hours only.


## References {.allowframebreaks}
